input.initialPrompt: Run coverage analysis on this project. Coverage data is already in TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml.

arguments.command: which dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report || echo "no reportgenerator found"

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to run coverage analysis on the project. They mention that coverage data is already in `TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml`. Let me look at what's available and generate a coverage report from that data.

arguments.command: which dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report || echo "no reportgenerator found"

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-1be7368a6ddb4c7999a14f371a0b17b5 && cat TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml | head -50", "description": "Inspect coverage XML file", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool list -g 2>/dev/null | grep -i report || echo \"no reportgenerator found\"", "description": "Check for report generator tool", "initial_wait": 10}

data.content:
Here's the coverage summary:

```
Coverage Report – ContosoUniversity
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Line coverage:     42.5%  (20/47 lines)
Branch coverage:   45%    (9/20 branches)
Method coverage:   100%   (4/4 methods)

Assembly Breakdown:
  ContosoUniversity.Services.StudentService  42.5%
    • Enroll()         – 75% line / 67% branch ✓
    • CalculateGpa()   – 25% line / 20% branch ✗
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
```

**Key finding:** `CalculateGpa` has very low coverage (25% line, 20% branch) — most of its logic is untested. `Enroll` is reasonably covered but has two uncovered branches (lines 17 and 23). The full report is at `TestResults/CoverageReport/Summary.txt`.
